title: ALLTRADE Calendar 2026 British Birds of Prey Large Premium Wall 2026 Calendar UK Holidays MADE IN BRITAIN® on FSC® Paper Photos by LAURIE CAMPBELL Supports The Woodland Trust Tree Planting Great Gift
features: ✅ 🇬🇧 2026 BRITISH BIRDS OF PREY CALENDAR: Features stunning images of UK's Woodlands, perfect for nature lovers and photography enthusiasts.
✅ 🇬🇧 ECO-FRIENDLY AND SUSTAINABLE: Made with FSC certified paper printed using vegetable inks reflecting a commitment to environmental sustainability and responsible forestry practices.
✅ 🇬🇧 SUPPORTING UK CONSERVATION: A portion of each sale goes to The Woodland Trust Carbon Capture Programme, aiding in reforestation and wildlife habitat preservation efforts across the UK.
✅ 🇬🇧 EDUCATIONAL AND INFORMATIVE: Offers insights into the heritage of British Woodlands making it an educational tool for all ages.
✅ 🇬🇧 PROUDLY PRODUCED IN UK: Locally designed and printed, ensuring high quality and supporting British businesses.
✅ 🇬🇧 FUNCTIONAL DESIGN: Includes public holidays, ample space for daily planning, and notes, making it practical for home or office use.

✅ 🇬🇧 EXCLUSIVE COLLECTOR'S ITEM: Each calendar is a special collector's piece, showcasing the rare beauty of Heritage Trees in the UK, and serves as a perfect and unique gift for nature lovers and Heritage Trees enthusiasts.
✅ 🇬🇧 VIBRANT AND DETAILED IMAGERY: High-resolution photography brings each Heritage Tree's character and beauty to life, enhancing any room.
✅ 🇬🇧 PROMOTES AWARENESS OF BRITISH WOODLANDS: Encourages appreciation and understanding of British Woodlands and the importance of conservation efforts.

✅ 🇬🇧 SUSTAINABLE PACKAGING: Embracing environmental responsibility, our calendar is packaged in a fully recyclable film, thoughtfully made with over 80% recycled content, showcasing our dedication to a greener future.
description: Product Description 2026 Calendars UK Holidays MADE IN BRITAIN on FSC Material & Carbon Capture with Trees Planted in UK by Woodland Trust Produced with 100% Solar Energy Thank you for your interest in our environmentally responsible range of calendars. We are committed to reducing our carbon footprint and supporting sustainable practices for manufacturing products with less miles. By planting trees all over Great Britain Woodlands, we aim to create natural havens for wildlife, provide carbon capture, and mitigate climate change. This is a Large Premium 2026 Wall Calendar. Photography by Laurie Campbell and Archie Miles British Made 2026 Calendars Premium Large Wall Calendar 2026 is a must for all to appreciate the beauty of Great Britain Miles Campbell Collection - 2026 Calendars Photographers Archie Miles & Laurie Campbell A bit about our British Photographers Laurie Campbell and Archie Miles LAURIE CAMPBELL With a life-long interest in the natural world and graduating with a degree in photography at Napier University, Laurie Campbell has been a freelance professional nature photographer, dedicated to documenting Scotland’s natural history for almost forty years. His imagery is published across a range of media internationally and is widely recognised for its distinctive style. Despite having produced and illustrated numerous books and winning competition awards, his most treasured accolade to date was to be voted for inclusion in ‘HighlandNaturalists’ an exhibition featuring thirty people from the past 300 years whose work has significantly contributed to the understanding and enjoyment of the natural history in the Scottish Highlands. Similarly, in 2014 he was honoured with a ‘Lifetime Achievement Award’ in the RSPB Nature of Scotland Awards in recognition of the lasting contribution his photography has made to conservation in Scotland. ARCHIE MILES Archie Miles has been a professional photographer for almost y years, and began writing almost thirty years ago. With an abiding passion for the British landscape, a natural progression over these last thirty years has seen him carve a specialist niche in the world of trees. He has written and photographed nine books on the subject and contributed images to many more books and publications. Archie has worked with several of the major tree-related organisations, including e Woodland Trust, Tree Council & Forestry Commission. His comprehensive picture library contains over 50,000 tree photographs and related historical archive material. He regularly presents illustrated talks for a variety of clients, and has occasionally worked on radio and television. He lives in rural Herefordshire with his partner Jan, Scotties “Dotty” and “Jessie” and “Eric” the cat. Calendar 2026 British Sunrises & Sunsets 2026 Calendar UK Holidays M...
